body.raw:
# ButtonGroup

You can use the `Stack` or `ButtonGroup` component to group buttons. When you use the `ButtonGroup` component, it allows you to:

- Set the `size` and `variant` of all buttons within it.
- Add `spacing` between the buttons.
- Flush the buttons together by removing the border radius of their children as needed.

## Import

```jsx live=false
import { ButtonGroup } from '@totejs/uikit';
```

## Usage

```jsx
<ButtonGroup size="md" variant="second">
  <Button colorScheme="warning">Test Button</Button>
  <IconButton icon={<SunIcon />}></IconButton>
</ButtonGroup>
```

## isAttached

```jsx
<ButtonGroup size="md" variant="scene" isAttached>
  <Button colorScheme="warning">Test Button</Button>
  <IconButton icon={<SunIcon />}></IconButton>
</ButtonGroup>
```

## Props

<PropsTable
  data={[
    {
      name: 'colorShceme',
      required: false,
      type: 'success | danger | warning | primary',
      default: 'primary',
      desc: 'When `variant="scene"`, you can change the skin of the button',
    },
    {
      name: 'size',
      required: false,
      type: 'sm | md | rg',
      default: 'rg',
      desc: '',
    },
    {
      name: 'variant',
      required: false,
      type: 'scene | brand | second | ghost | text | link',
      default: 'scene',
      desc: '',
    },
    {
      name: 'isDisabled',
      required: false,
      type: 'boolean',
      default: false,
      desc: 'If true, the button will be disabled.',
    },
    {
      name: 'isAttached',
      required: false,
      type: 'boolean',
      default: false,
      desc: 'If true, the borderRadius of button that are direct children will be altered to look flushed together',
    },
    {
      name: 'spacing',
      required: false,
      type: 'number | string',
      default: false,
      desc: 'The spacing between the buttons',
    },
  ]}
/>
